codeReview.summary: The experiment detail view overlay is well-implemented in dashboard/app.py with comprehensive coverage of all VAL-TABLE assertions (011-017). The code is clean, well-structured, and handles all required edge cases. The _build_metric_card helper reduces duplication effectively. All 25 tests pass.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The dashboard has a separate venv at dashboard/.venv/ distinct from the project root .venv/. The services.yaml test command correctly uses dashboard/.venv/bin/python. Workers need to be aware that dashboard tests must use dashboard/.venv, not the project root .venv or system python.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/services.yaml:3 — test command uses dashboard/.venv/bin/python; pyproject.toml does not list dash/plotly as dependencies because they live in the dashboard subproject.

summary: Solid implementation of the experiment detail view overlay. The feature covers all 7 validation assertions (VAL-TABLE-011 through VAL-TABLE-017): experiment ID header, key metrics at 4 decimal places, conditional later-era metrics with N/A fallback, full hypothesis text, params key-value display, CV section with fold deltas and bar charts, and snapshot mini-charts with 3 distinguishable colored lines. Edge cases for validation_failed (error banner + N/A metrics), no_results, missing files, and old-era experiments are all handled. Code quality is good — _build_metric_card helper eliminates the prior inline duplication, error display is prominent with pre-wrap for multi-line messages, and the fold scores bar chart includes a mean reference line. All 25 tests pass. No blocking or non-blocking issues found.
